english: tribute Lex.; to commission, to charge with, to make, appoint, constitute, *mi żig gád-po-la kál ċe* (mi zhig rgad po la bkal ce) Ld. to appoint some one to be an elder or senior, cf _čól-ba ('col ba).' — 2. to put, to place on or over, gdúṅ-ma bkál-ba (gdung ma bkal ba) a beam placed over it S.g.; to set or put on, e.g. a pot on a trevet; to hang up, gos-gel-gdáṅ (gos 'gel gdang) a stand to hang clothes on; fig. _či-bar nús-pai tog _gel dgos ('chi bar nus pa'i tog 'gel dgos) one must set on it the roof of being able to die, i.e. one must crown the whole edifice by being free from fear of death Mil.

pf. bkas (bkas), fut. dgas (dgas), imp. kos (khos), trs. to _gás-pa ('gas pa), to split, cleave, divide. bkas-śiṅ (bkas shing) Lex. cleft or chopped wood; dúm-bur (dum bur) (to divide) into pieces Lex., to cut up or open, e.g. a fish, gourd, pumpkin, Dzl.

= mgo (mgo) in some figurative applications of the word: dmág-_go (dmag 'go) commander of an army Cs.; mkár-_go (mkhar 'go), rdzóṅ-_go (rdzong 'go) commander of a fort Cs.; _go-snám ('go snam) a sort of fine cloth made of shawl-wool, or also: Europe-cloth, i.e. broad cloth = sag-lád (sag lad); _gó-pa ('go pa) officer, captain, head-man of a village or district, esp. in W.; in a general sense: *koṅ-čóg ǰig-ten-gyi gó-pa yin* (dkon mchog 'jig rten gyi 'go pa yin) God is the ruler of the world; *koṅ-čóg-gi saṅ gó-pa med* (dkon mchog gi sang 'go pa med) God is the only and highest ruler; *_go-pón* ('go dpon) C. rector, director, head-master, principal e.g. of a school; _gó-ma ('go ma) Zam. beginning, origin, source; _gó-mi ('go mi) Lex. = _gó-pa ('go pa); *_go-yód* ('go yod) = _gó-pa ('go pa) Ld.; _gor ('gor) in the beginning, at first, originally Sch., sér-bai _gor (ser ba'i 'gor) when it began to hail Mil.nt.

pf gos (gos) (or _gos ('gos)), cf. bsgo-ba (bsgo ba), 1. to stain, to lose colour; to dirty, sully one's self, dé-la (de la) with it, naṅ-skyúgs lús-la (nang skyugs lus la) to soil one's self with vomit. — 2. to infect, with a disease, _gó-bai nad ('go ba'i nad), _go-nád ('go nad), _gó-bai rims ('go ba'i rims), a contagious or epidemic disease, a plague, frq.

pf. bkog (bkog), fut. dgog (dgog)? imp. kog (kog) 1. to take away forcibly, to snatch, tear away, pull out, rtsá-ba (rtsa ba) a root Lex., so a tooth Schr.; to tear up, e.g. a floor W.; to peel Sch.; *kóg-te kyér-ċe* (bkog ste khyer ce) W. to rob, plunder frq.: *kóg-te kyers* (bkog ste khyers) Ld. it has been robbed. — 2. to take off, a cover, a lid. a pot from the fire W.

another form for _gégs-pa ('gegs pa), to prevent, to avert unfortunate events, fatal consequences; to suppress, the symptoms of a disease by medicine; to drive back or away, to expel e.g. spirits, ghosts; to repel people that are trying to land.

1 Cs.: to bewitch, enchant (?), _góṅ-ba-po ('gong ba po), _góṅ-po ('gong po) an enchanter, sorcerer, _góṅ-ba-mo ('gong ba mo) enchantress, sorceress Cs.; more frq. _góṅ-po ('gong po) an evil spirit, demon, also fig. demon of concupiscence, of fear, of terror Mil.; _góṅ-mo ('gong mo) fem. — 2. pf. bkoṅ (bkong), perh. more corr. sgóṅ-ba (sgong ba), spá-sgoṅ-ba (spa sgong ba) Lex. to despond.

pf. bgod (bgod), fut. dgod (dgod), imp. Kod (khod) (cf. _kód-pa ('khod pa)), the Latin _condere_, 1. to design, to project, to plan Schr. — 2. to found, to etablish, to lay out (a town), to build (a house); hence bkód-pai rig-byéd (bkod pa'i rig byed) books on architecture Glr.; to manufacture, to form, to frame. — 3. to put, to fix, to transfer, into a certain state or condition, bdé(-ba)-la (bde (ba) la) Dzl., bder (bder) Lex., into a happy state, dge-ba-la (dge ba la) Dzl. into virtue, čós-la (chos la) Pth. into the true doctrine, rnám-par gról-ba-la (rnam par grol ba la) Dzl. into salvation, mya-ṅán-las _dás-pa-la (mya ngan las 'das pa la) into delivery from existence Dzl.; żag-gráṅs (zhag grangs) to fix a certain time or term Schr.; tsad (tshad) (to determine) the measure or size of a thing Schr. — 4. to set, put, or place in order, gral-pyám bgód-pa _dra (gral phyam bgod pa 'dra) as the rafters of a roof are placed side by side S.g.; mtar dgód-pa (mthar dgod pa) to add or affix e.g. ciphers to a certain number Wdk.; bkód-par mdzés-pá (bkod par mdzes pa) beautiful as to arrangement, nicely ordered, (b)rgyan dgód-pa ((b)rgyan dgod pa) Lex. to arrange ornaments (tastefully), to decorate, adorn, to construct or adjust grammatical forms, sentences Zam. — 5. to put down in writing, to record, miṅ ká-ba-la (ming ka ba la) to write names on a column Pth.; to compose, draw up, write, a narrative etc., frq.; to mention, to insert, in a writing; *ka kó-pa* (bka' bkod pa) C. to publish, to make known. — 6. to rule, to govern Schr.; byol-sóṅ bkód-pai rgyál- (byol song bkod pa'i rgyal)
